You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Cleaning a spoon and putting it away.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description for "Cleaning a spoon and putting it away":

1. Objects and their types:
   - Receptacles:
     - Sink: A double-basin stainless steel sink with two faucets, embedded in a brown countertop. It is a receptacle and is openable (though not shown opened or closed in the images).
     - Microwave: A dark red microwave oven with a digital display showing "OFF", mounted above a countertop. It is a receptacle and is openable.
     - Cabinet: A beige cabinet under the sink with two doors. It is a receptacle and is openable.
   - Objects:
     - Spoon: A metallic spoon is visible on a white table to the left of the microwave. It is not currently in any receptacle.
     - Knife: A knife is also visible on the same white table next to the spoon. It is an object of type “knife” as defined in the domain.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 sink (receptacle)
   - 1 microwave (receptacle)
   - 1 cabinet (receptacle)
   - 1 spoon (object)
   - 1 knife (object)

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The spoon is on the white table, which is to the left of the microwave.
   - The microwave is mounted above a countertop, to the right of the table.
   - The sink is embedded in the countertop, directly below the microwave.
   - The cabinet is under the sink.
   - The knife is on the same table as the spoon, adjacent to it.

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - The spoon is not held by the agent and is not in any receptacle.
   - The spoon is not clean (isClean is false).
   - The microwave is off (isOn is false).
   - The sink is not currently being used for cleaning (no object is in it or being cleaned).
   - The cabinet doors are closed (opened is false).
   - The agent is not at any object’s location initially.

5. Colors and visual properties:
   - The sink is metallic silver with a brushed finish and has two basins.
   - The countertop is brown with a marbled texture.
   - The microwave is dark red with a black control panel and digital display.
   - The cabinet is beige with brown handles.
   - The table is white with black metal legs.
   - The spoon is metallic and reflective.
   - The knife has a metallic blade and a dark handle.

Note: The spoon must be picked up, cleaned in the sink, and then placed in a receptacle (likely the cabinet or microwave, though the cabinet is more appropriate for storage). The agent must navigate to the table to pick up the spoon, then to the sink to clean it, and finally to the cabinet to put it away.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem clean_spoon_and_put_away)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        microwave1 - microwave
        cabinet1 - receptacle
        spoon1 - object
        knife1 - knife
        table1 - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 spoon1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 table1))

        ; Receptacles are openable
        (openable sink1)
        (openable microwave1)
        (openable cabinet1)

        ; Cabinet is closed initially
        (not (opened cabinet1))

        ; Spoon is on the table, not held, not clean
        (atLocation agent1 table1) ; Assuming agent starts near table for simplicity, as per scene
        (inReceptacle spoon1 table1)
        (not (holds agent1 spoon1))
        (not (isClean spoon1))

        ; Knife is on the table
        (inReceptacle knife1 table1)

        ; Microwave is off
        (not (isOn microwave1))

        ; Sink and cabinet are not being used for cleaning or storage
        (not (isClean spoon1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isClean spoon1)
        (inReceptacle spoon1 cabinet1)
    ))
)
```

(define (problem clean_spoon_and_put_away)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        microwave1 - microwave
        cabinet1 - receptacle
        spoon1 - object
        knife1 - knife
        table1 - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 spoon1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 table1))

        ; Receptacles are openable
        (openable sink1)
        (openable microwave1)
        (openable cabinet1)

        ; Cabinet is closed initially
        (not (opened cabinet1))

        ; Spoon is on the table, not held, not clean
        (atLocation agent1 table1) ; Assuming agent starts near table for simplicity, as per scene
        (inReceptacle spoon1 table1)
        (not (holds agent1 spoon1))
        (not (isClean spoon1))

        ; Knife is on the table
        (inReceptacle knife1 table1)

        ; Microwave is off
        (not (isOn microwave1))

        ; Sink and cabinet are not being used for cleaning or storage
        (not (isClean spoon1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isClean spoon1)
        (inReceptacle spoon1 cabinet1)
    ))
)

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a kitchen environment with several objects relevant to the "put_task" domain. The primary focus is on the sink, microwave, and a knife, along with a spoon that is the target of the cleaning task.

**Objects and Their Types:**
- **Receptacles:**
  - **Sink**: A double-basin stainless steel sink embedded in a brown countertop. It is a receptacle capable of holding objects and is used for cleaning. It is positioned against the back wall, with beige cabinets directly beneath it.
  - **Microwave**: A dark red microwave oven mounted above the countertop to the right of the sink. It is a receptacle that can hold objects for heating. Its control panel displays "OFF", indicating it is currently not powered on.
  - **Cabinets**: The beige cabinets under the sink are openable receptacles, though they appear empty in the images.

- **Objects (non-receptacles):**
  - **Knife**: A metallic knife is visible on a small white table to the left of the microwave. It is an object used for slicing.
  - **Spoon**: A golden spoon is resting on the same white table, next to the knife. This is the object that needs to be cleaned and put away.
  - **Table**: A small white table with black metal legs is positioned to the left of the microwave. It holds the knife and spoon.

**Quantities:**
- 1 Sink (receptacle)
- 1 Microwave (receptacle)
- 1 Knife (object)
- 1 Spoon (object)
- 1 Table (object, serving as a surface)
- 2 Cabinets (receptacles, under the sink)

**Spatial Relationships:**
- The spoon and knife are on the white table, which is to the left of the microwave.
- The microwave is mounted above the countertop, to the right of the sink.
- The sink is centrally located on the countertop, with cabinets directly underneath.
- The floor is tiled with brown square tiles, and the walls are a light beige color.

**Current State of Objects:**
- The microwave is currently off (isOn ?microwave is false).
- The cabinets under the sink are closed (opened ?cabinet is false).
- The spoon is not currently held by any agent and is not clean (isClean ?spoon is false).
- The knife is not currently held by any agent.
- No object is currently in any receptacle (inReceptacle ?o ?r is false for all objects and receptacles).

**Visual Properties:**
- The sink is shiny and metallic, with two basins and a faucet with two handles.
- The microwave is dark red with a digital display and control panel.
- The spoon is golden and reflective, placed on the white table.
- The knife has a metallic blade and a dark handle, also on the white table.
- The countertop is brown with a marbled texture.
- The cabinets are beige with simple horizontal handles.

This scene description provides a comprehensive overview of the objects, their types, quantities, spatial relationships, current states, and visual properties, focusing on those relevant to the domain and the instruction to clean a spoon and put it away.

    ))
)

You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

(define (problem clean_spoon)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        microwave1 - microwave
        cabinet1 - receptacle
        cabinet2 - receptacle
        knife1 - knife
        spoon1 - object
        table1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 spoon1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 table1))
        (openable cabinet1)
        (openable cabinet2)
        (not (opened cabinet1))
        (not (opened cabinet2))
        (not (isClean spoon1))
        (not (isHot spoon1))
        (not (isCool spoon1))
        (not (isOn microwave1))
        (not (isToggled microwave1))
        (not (isSliced spoon1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 spoon1))
        (not (holds agent1 knife1))
        (inReceptacle spoon1 table1)
        (inReceptacle knife1 table1)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isClean spoon1)
        (inReceptacle spoon1 cabinet1)
    ))
)
